themes: the road that unmakes the unprepared

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=88 beats='second person; you are a wanderer at the head of the frost causeway, the salt desert that unmakes the unprepared; the shrine lies a hard crossing away across a waste that has kept far more pilgrims than it has passed; grave stakes, stated plainly: the road is one line of markers and one law, walk it exactly or the salt has you'>>

<<FILL role=setup words=82 beats='the last treeline falls behind; the white waste opens flat and endless; the one rule of the frost causeway is stated here: there is no water you did not carry and no way back, only the next marker and the next, all the way across or not at all'>>

choices.label: Line up on the first marker.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=80 beats='you take the pilgrim's stance the road demands: eyes low, water counted, every step read before it is trusted; the first hazard stands ahead, and the salt is already testing whether you are one it will keep'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=76 beats='the road reaches the Rime Threshold; the danger is stated plainly in the ground itself: a thin salt crust laid over black standing brine; it bears weight only where the old stakes mark the firm shelf, and the white sheen between is a lid over drowning'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=64 beats='the mistake lands: the crust gives under the first full step and the brine takes your leg to the hip'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=62 beats='one held breath in which the whole road narrows to this; the white lid tilts and the black water shows at its edge; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=68 beats='at the Threshold Crust the road closes: the runner-thin lid folds you into the cold black beneath it and closes white again over the place; the salt keeps it, quick and without dwelling'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=64 beats='the mistake lands: you land short and the seam is not a seam but an open mouth of brine'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=62 beats='one held breath in which the whole road narrows to this; the white lid tilts and the black water shows at its edge; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=68 beats='at the Threshold Crust the road closes: the salt water is very old and very still and it keeps what falls into it without a sound; the salt keeps it, quick and without dwelling'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=64 beats='the mistake lands: you stand and wait, and the tide of brine under the pan rises instead of falling'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=62 beats='there is a moment here you could still move, and then it is gone; delay is death at the Threshold Crust: the white lid tilts and the black water shows at its edge, and the time you spent waiting is the thing that kills you'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=68 beats='at the Threshold Crust the road closes: delay is death on the crust: it does not dry, it softens, and the ground you trusted is water by the time you move; the salt keeps it, quick and without dwelling'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=64 beats='the mistake lands: flat and spread you still crack the lid, and spread you cannot pull free'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=62 beats='one held breath in which the whole road narrows to this; the white lid tilts and the black water shows at its edge; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=68 beats='at the Threshold Crust the road closes: the crust takes the clever the same as the careless, slowly and without any cruelty drawn out; the salt keeps it, quick and without dwelling'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=92 beats='here is the crossing of the Rime Threshold; the safe way is narrow and the wrong ways are many, and the road has stated which is which; a thin salt crust laid over black standing brine; it bears weight only where the old stakes mark the firm shelf, and the white sheen between is a lid over drowning; choose, and be right'>>

choices.label: Follow the staked rope-line across the firm shelf.
Cut straight over the white sheen to save the walk.
Jump the dark seams between the firm plates.
Wait for the noon sun to dry the thin crust hard.
Crawl to spread your weight over the thinnest stretch.
Gamble it and press on against the Rime Threshold.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=64 beats='past the Rime Threshold the frost causeway lets you on; the stakes hold true; the shelf bears you and the frost causeway lets you onto it; the shrine is a little nearer and the crossing a little longer survived'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=80 beats='you have gambled and pressed into the Rime Threshold without the safe line; for a breath the salt has not taken you, and three ways open, only one of them onward: the white lid tilts and the black water shows at its edge'>>

choices.label: Hold your nerve and slip on past the Rime Threshold while you still can.
Push your luck harder and drive on through the Rime Threshold.
Falter, and let the Rime Threshold turn you from the road.

<<FILL role=rising words=64 beats='the mistake lands: the water you hoarded rides you down into the soft weighing-pan at the door'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=62 beats='one held breath in which the whole road narrows to this; the skin at your side is wrong by the weight of a life; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=62 beats='one held breath in which the whole road narrows to this; the skin at your side is wrong by the weight of a life; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=62 beats='there is a moment here you could still move, and then it is gone; delay is death at the Weighing House: the skin at your side is wrong by the weight of a life, and the time you spent waiting is the thing that kills you'>>

choices.label: Take the pilgrim's measure exactly and seal it.
Fill every skin to the brim against the thirst ahead.
Drink your fill now and go out light and fast.
Trade a ration to a guide for a charm of safe passage.
Rest a full day at the well to gather your strength.
Gamble it and press on against the Weigh Station.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=76 beats='the road reaches the Blind Noon; the danger is stated plainly in the ground itself: the salt pan throws the sun back doubled; unshaded eyes burn to blindness by noon, and no blind pilgrim has ever crossed the frost causeway'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=64 beats='the mistake lands: the glare off the pan sears the sight out of you before the shrine is any nearer'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=62 beats='one held breath in which the whole road narrows to this; the white comes up into your eyes and does not leave them; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=68 beats='at the White Noon the road closes: the White Noon takes the eyes first and the pilgrim after, for the blind wander off the road and are not found; the salt keeps it, quick and without dwelling'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=64 beats='the mistake lands: one long look into the doubled light and the world goes white and stays white'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=62 beats='one held breath in which the whole road narrows to this; the white comes up into your eyes and does not leave them; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=68 beats='at the White Noon the road closes: the noon glare does not give the eyes back; you stand blind on a road you can no longer see; the salt keeps it, quick and without dwelling'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=64 beats='the mistake lands: haste in the glare is stumbling, and stumbling on the salt opens you to the light and the heat both'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=62 beats='one held breath in which the whole road narrows to this; the white comes up into your eyes and does not leave them; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=68 beats='at the White Noon the road closes: the pan is wider than your speed; the doubled noon finds the hurrying and puts them down; the salt keeps it, quick and without dwelling'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=64 beats='the mistake lands: you lie down on the white pan and the heat coming off it does not pass, it gathers'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=62 beats='there is a moment here you could still move, and then it is gone; delay is death at the White Noon: the white comes up into your eyes and does not leave them, and the time you spent waiting is the thing that kills you'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=68 beats='at the White Noon the road closes: delay is death at the noon: the still on the open salt are cooked where they wait, eyes shut or open; the salt keeps it, quick and without dwelling'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=92 beats='here is the crossing of the Blind Noon; the safe way is narrow and the wrong ways are many, and the road has stated which is which; the salt pan throws the sun back doubled; unshaded eyes burn to blindness by noon, and no blind pilgrim has ever crossed the frost causeway; choose, and be right'>>

choices.label: Bind the smoked-glass slit over your eyes and read the ground by feel.
Walk with eyes open to keep the far shrine in sight.
Look up to fix the sun's angle and your heading.
Press on hard to be past the pan before full noon.
Lie still and shut your eyes until the noon passes.
Gamble it and press on against the Blind Noon.

choices.label: Wrap the face-cloth tight and crawl the leeward furrow.
Stand and push through the wind upright.
Turn your back and wait the scouring out.
Run before the wind to let it carry you fast.
Unwrap your face to breathe more easily.
Shelter in the lee of a drift and dig in deep.
Gamble it and press on against the Howling Drift.

choices.label: Skirt the discolored ground along the ridge of hard salt.
Cross the smooth white directly, straightest and fairest.
Probe ahead with your staff and trust where it holds.
Wait at the edge for the ground to firm at dusk.
Leap the narrow neck of the white.
Gamble it and press on against the Sink of Ice.

choices.label: Keep the cairn-and-bone line dead ahead and touch nothing.
Cut across to a nearer marker off the line.
Gather relics from the dead for the shrine.
Follow a fresher set of tracks off the marked way.
Sit down among the dead to rest a while.
Gamble it and press on against the Bone Cairn Field.

choices.label: Fill full, drink full, and mark the true onward line before leaving.
Hurry on without filling, the shrine in sight.
Drink until you can barely walk.
Fill up but take a shorter unmarked line.
Rest long at the water, safe at last.
Gamble it and press on against the Last Spring.

Falter, and let the Last Spring turn you from the road.

Run for the shrine the moment you see it.

Gamble it and press on against the Final Mile.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=78 beats='the salt steps of the Iron Monastery stand out of the waste at last, pale and plain against the sky; the frost causeway has one hazard more, the keeping of its own threshold, and it is as grave as any behind you'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the shrine admits only the prepared; the danger at the steps is stated as plainly as the rest of the road: the pilgrim who forces the threshold, or collapses on it, or turns from the rite at the last, does not enter, and the salt at the door keeps its own the same as the open waste did'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=64 beats='the mistake lands: you throw yourself up the salt steps and past the rite, spent and heedless, and the threshold does not take the forced'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=62 beats='one held breath in which the whole crossing narrows to this last door; the shrine is right there and closed against you; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving'>>
<<FILL role=failure words=68 beats='at the threshold the road closes: the forced door keeps the pilgrim on the steps, a stride from sanctuary and no nearer; the salt keeps it, quick and grave'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=90 beats='the keeper's rite waits at the top of the steps; the shrine has stated its one law at the door, the same austere law as the whole pale road: give the pilgrim's measure and kneel, or be kept; choose, and be right at the last'>>

choices.label: Give the pilgrim's measure and kneel to the rite.
Force past the rite and into the shrine.
Turn from the rite at the very threshold.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=66 beats='at the very door you turn from the rite, and to turn here is to turn back onto the waste with nothing left to cross it; unmade within sight of sanctuary, you become the last the pale road kept'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=66 beats='you give the measure and kneel, and the rite is answered; the keeper draws the threshold open and the cool dark of the shrine breathes out over you; the frost causeway is behind you and one thing only remains, how you come in'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=86 beats='the Iron Monastery is open and you are alive at its door, which few are; the crossing has left its mark on you and how you cross the threshold is the last of the road; choose how the frost causeway delivers you'>>

choices.label: Cross the threshold whole, walking in on your own feet.
Cross broken but alive, dragged in by the last of your will.
Cross by giving yourself over to the shrine's keeping.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=78 beats='you walk into the Iron Monastery on your own feet, water still in you and the road read true from the first marker to the last; the salt did not keep you; you are the rare pilgrim it passed, whole'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=78 beats='you come into the shrine broken by the crossing, blind-eyed or bled or spent to nothing, but alive and inside; the frost causeway took its price and let you keep the rest; you arrived, and arriving is everything'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=76 beats='you cross the last threshold by giving yourself to the shrine's keeping, the crossing made and the self that made it laid down at the end of it; the frost causeway is answered, grave and complete, in the only coin it takes'>>
